parsed_review.summary:
CP00-612 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only continuation of the RP20 Data Room And VDR P01 queue (RP20.P01.M04.S07–RP20.P01.M06.S04, risk B, 40 units). Read-only inspection of registry.js/service.js/validators.js, the contract projections, the test suite, README, package.json wiring, and the live closeout-pack-plan confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is supported.

Verified: (1) Section rows produce exactly 40 units across 3 micro-sections (M04=14 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05=22 Permission And Audit Binding, M06=4 Synthetic Fixture Set); per-row deliverable-type mapping was manually recomputed and equals the plan's per-unit types and the validator's expected counts {implementation:22, ui:6, fixture:2, test:6, hermes_evidence:2, claude_review:2} = 40. (2) The M04 boundary against CP611 (S01–S06 in CP611, S07–S20 in CP612) is clean and non-overlapping. (3) contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json cp612_pack_binding, cp612_requirements, cp612_no_write_attestation, and the cp612 descriptor projection match the registry constants the model.test.js deep-equals assert against. (4) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensive: freezeCp612Result and the no-write attestation set all data-room/vdr/rfi/cp/closing-binder/access-analytics runtime, permission-decision, audit-trace, object-storage, fixture, and real-data flags to false; CP612 validators additionally assert writes_permission_decision/appends_audit_trace/loads_real_fixture_data are false. (5) Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; validators only ever return production_ready_candidate, never a final ready claim, preserving the production_ready gate. (6) Hermes usage is deterministic descriptor packet/receipt shaping only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. (7) Review hardening is satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte stdout), prompt is 2236 B (<25600) with no embedded diff/source, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools.

The pending evidence files (command-evidence.json commands:[], adjudication.md pending, construction-inspection.json PENDING_REVIEW, manifest production_ready:false/status:in_progress) are the correct pre-adjudication state for the read_only_claude_run_raw_capture step; they are populated downstream in the hardening sequence (normalize → receipt_validate → finding_adjudication → construction_inspection → final_closeout_validation → commit). Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, LDIP integration JSON) are correctly out of pack scope and were not treated as part of CP00-612.

Limitation: as a read-only reviewer I cannot execute the closeout validation command matrix (npm test, npm run validate, etc.); source inspection indicates these would pass, but final pack closeout remains contingent on that matrix completing at exit 0 plus human final approval — Claude is an independent reviewer, not the final approver. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
